titel: Stricter requirements for adult education in Swedish for immigrants (SFI).
html:
The proposal includes suggested amendments to the Education Act (2010:800), which will tighten the requirements for municipal adult education in Swedish for immigrants (SFI). The proposed changes are as follows:

  • The right to participate in SFI will be valid for three years from the date a person is first admitted to the program, with certain possibilities for extension.
  • The municipality must create an action plan for its efforts to reach individuals in the municipality who are eligible for SFI.
  • Individuals eligible for SFI will have the right to participate in the program in a municipality other than their home municipality, if they have special reasons based on their personal circumstances. A municipality that accepts a student from another municipality for SFI will receive compensation from the student's home municipality to cover the costs of the student's education.
  • When a person is admitted to SFI, their knowledge should be assessed, unless such an assessment is obviously unnecessary. The results of the assessment should form the basis for the individual study plan.
  • The individual study plan for a student in SFI must include the date when the student was first admitted to the program. If a student transfers to SFI with a different provider, the provider the student is leaving must provide a copy of the student's individual study plan to the receiving provider.

The changes to the Education Act will come into force on January 1, 2026. For individuals who were already admitted to SFI before the effective date, the right to participate in the program will be valid for three years from the effective date, with the possibility of extension.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

The government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Education Act (2010:800).
  2. The Parliament approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2025:729) amending the Education Act (2010:800).
tldr: New regulations are being proposed to change how Swedish for Immigrants (SFI) education operates. The most important changes include limiting the amount of time individuals can participate in SFI to three years, and requiring municipalities to actively seek out and enroll eligible students.
lattlast:
The government wants to amend the law regarding education to make the requirements for Swedish for Immigrants (SFI) clearer. Starting January 1, 2026, the right to attend SFI will be valid for three years from the start of the program. However, there are possibilities to extend this period if necessary.

Municipalities must also create a plan for how they will reach all individuals who are eligible for SFI instruction. If a person has special circumstances, for example, due to their personal situation, they should be able to attend SFI in a municipality other than the one they live in. The municipality that accepts the student will then be compensated by the municipality where the person resides.

When a person begins SFI, the municipality must assess the person's knowledge of Swedish. The results of this assessment will be used to create a personalized study plan. This plan should indicate when the person started the program and must be included if the student transfers to another municipality.

For those already participating in SFI before the new rules come into effect, the right to participate in the program will be valid for three years from the date the new rules begin on January 1, 2026. Again, there are possibilities to extend this period.
